result.preview: "Execution successful. Output:\nNumber of cases for product in last 5 months: 2\nMost frequent issue: a03Wt00000JqnHwIAJ"

args.code:
import pandas as pd

# Get the order items for the product
order_items = pd.DataFrame(env['data_2'])
order_items['Id'] = order_items['Id'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()
order_items['Product2Id'] = order_items['Product2Id'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()

# Get the cases
cases = pd.DataFrame(env['data_4'])
cases['orderitemid__c'] = cases['orderitemid__c'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()
cases['issueid__c'] = cases['issueid__c'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()
cases['createddate'] = pd.to_datetime(cases['createddate'])

# Filter cases for the past 5 months from 2023-01-16
start_date = pd.to_datetime('2023-01-16') - pd.DateOffset(months=5)
end_date = pd.to_datetime('2023-01-16')

# Make createddate timezone naive for comparison
cases['createddate'] = cases['createddate'].dt.tz_localize(None)

filtered_cases = cases[(cases['createddate'] >= start_date) & (cases['createddate'] <= end_date)]

# Merge cases with order items to filter by product
merged = pd.merge(filtered_cases, order_items, left_on='orderitemid__c', right_on='Id', how='inner')

print(f"Number of cases for product in last 5 months: {len(merged)}")

if len(merged) > 0:
    most_frequent_issue = merged['issueid__c'].value_counts().index[0]
    print(f"Most frequent issue: {most_frequent_issue}")
else:
    print("No cases found.")

import pandas as pd

# Get the order items for the product
order_items = pd.DataFrame(env['data_2'])
order_items['Id'] = order_items['Id'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()
order_items['Product2Id'] = order_items['Product2Id'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()

# Get the cases
cases = pd.DataFrame(env['data_4'])
cases['orderitemid__c'] = cases['orderitemid__c'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()
cases['issueid__c'] = cases['issueid__c'].str.replace('#', '').str.strip()
cases['createddate'] = pd.to_datetime(cases['createddate'])

# Filter cases for the past 5 months from 2023-01-16
start_date = pd.to_datetime('2023-01-16') - pd.DateOffset(months=5)
end_date = pd.to_datetime('2023-01-16')

# Make createddate timezone naive for comparison
cases['createddate'] = cases['createddate'].dt.tz_localize(None)

filtered_cases = cases[(cases['createddate'] >= start_date) & (cases['createddate'] <= end_date)]

# Merge cases with order items to filter by product
merged = pd.merge(filtered_cases, order_items, left_on='orderitemid__c', right_on='Id', how='inner')

print(f"Number of cases for product in last 5 months: {len(merged)}")

if len(merged) > 0:
    most_frequent_issue = merged['issueid__c'].value_counts().index[0]
    print(f"Most frequent issue: {most_frequent_issue}")
else:
    print("No cases found.")
